name: Python for Beginners
description: Python syntax basics, data types, conditions, loops, functions and file handling.
label: PY

content.text: Python is an interpreted, high-level programming language with an emphasis on code readability. It uses indentation instead of curly braces to define code blocks, enforcing a clean and consistent structure.

Python is a dynamically typed language — variables do not need a declared type. Core types include int, float, str, bool, list, tuple, dict, and set. Type conversions are performed with built-in functions such as int(), str(), and float().

if/elif/else conditions allow program branching. A for loop iterates over sequences (lists, strings, range), while a while loop repeats as long as a condition is true. The break and continue statements control loop flow.

Functions are defined with the def keyword. Python supports default parameter values, *args for a variable number of arguments, and **kwargs for keyword arguments. Lambda expressions allow anonymous single-line functions.
